revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:含アルミニウム泉]]
[[File:Myoban Onsen 02.jpg|thumb|right|Example of an aluminum-containing spring, {{ill|Sinter Hut|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter Hut|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=|lt=Sinter Hut}} at {{ill|Myōban Onsen|ja|明礬温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōban Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別府温泉|1=Beppu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4890932}}]] '''Aluminum-Containing Springs''' (含アルミニウム泉) is a classification of {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}}<nowiki/>water quality based on the displayed spring quality names. It falls under the category of {{ill|therapeutic hot springs|ja|温泉#療養泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic hot springs|1=Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q655311}} that contain special components. == Overview == As the name suggests, this type of spring contains [[:en:Aluminum|aluminum]] [[:en:Ions|ions]]. Most of the springs previously classified under the old name '''Myōban-sen''' (明礬泉) fall into this category. It is said to be effective for {{ill|Skin_condition|en|Skin_condition|qid=Q949302|lt=skin conditions|1=Skin condition}} (details below). == Definition of Spring Quality == A hot spring containing at least 1,000 mg of dissolved components per kilogram of water, including at least 100 mg of aluminum ions (Al{{sup|3+|qq=}} ) as a {{ill|cation|en|cation|qid=Q326277|lt=cation|1=Cation}} , and sulfate ions (SO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup>) as an {{ill|anion|en|anion|qid=Q107968|lt=anion|1=Anion}}
== Comparison of New and Old Spring Classifications == The following table shows the comparison between new and old classifications: {| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of New and Old Spring Classifications<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |title=参考資料２新旧泉質名対照 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=環境省自然環境局自然環境整備課温泉地保護利用推進室 |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! scope="col" id="旧泉質名" | Old Spring Name ! scope="col" id="新泉質名" | New Spring Name ! scope="col" id="略記泉質名" | Abbreviated Spring Name |- | Containing Myōban・Ryokuban Springs (含明礬・緑礬泉) | {{Plainlist| * Aluminum・Iron(II)–Sulfate Spring (アルミニウム・鉄（Ⅱ）－硫酸塩泉) * Or Iron(II)-Containing・Aluminum–Sulfate Spring (含鉄（Ⅱ）－アルミニウム－硫酸塩泉) }} | {{Plainlist| * Al･Fe(Ⅱ)-SO<sub>4</sub>泉 * 含Fe(Ⅱ)-Al-SO<sub>4</sub>泉 }} |} Containing Myōban・Ryokuban Springs are also compared to {{ill|Chalybeate|en|Chalybeate|qid=Q3046709|lt=Chalybeate springs|1=Chalybeate springs}} , but Aluminum-Containing Springs are classified separately based on the amount of aluminum ions present. == Effects == ''※ The effects are not guaranteed for all individuals.'' The following benefits are attributed to this type of spring: * Bathing: In addition to general benefits, it is said to help with chronic skin diseases. * Drinking: It is believed to aid in chronic {{ill|Human digestive system|en|Human digestive system|qid=Q9649|lt=digestive system|1=Human digestive system}}<nowiki/>disorders. == Representative Hot Springs == * {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|en|Kusatsu Onsen|qid=Q3137477|lt=Kusatsu Onsen|1=Kusatsu Onsen}}(Gunma Prefecture) * {{ill|Tamagawa Hot Spring|en|Tamagawa Hot Spring|qid=Q7680539|lt=Tamagawa Onsen (Akita)|1=Tamagawa Hot Spring}}(Akita Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} {{Onsen|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|含アルミニウム泉|version=101423886|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:含アルミニウム泉]]
